analysis: The overall market sentiment is bullish, favoring bullish reversal setups. Among bullish candidates, MARUTI.NS shows the strongest signal strength (8) and highest volume ratio (2.11), indicating strong buying interest and momentum, making it the top bullish pick. M&M.NS also has a solid volume ratio (1.46), good RSI (59.7), and a strong percent change (4.58%), supporting its selection as the second bullish pick. TATACONSUM.NS, despite being bullish, has lower signal strength and volume ratio compared to these two. For bearish picks, POWERGRID.NS has a decent volume ratio (1.16) and a turning score of 4, but low signal strength (1), indicating a weaker bearish conviction; however, it is still chosen due to lack of stronger bearish candidates. BHARTIARTL.NS has moderate signal strength (4) but volume ratio below 1, suggesting less conviction. Both bearish picks have stop losses set above buy prices to limit risk, and targets are set with realistic one-day risk-reward ratios. The bullish picks align well with the bullish market sentiment, while bearish picks are chosen cautiously given the overall positive market bias.
